summary: Student productivity app named 'Tracky' for tracking syllabus progress, study time, and chapter completion with user authentication. Students can add subjects, and within each subject, add multiple chapters with completion tracking. Progress is calculated per-subject and overall, with automatic report generation.
architectural_notes: Database must enforce per-user data isolation
Dashboard aggregates data from syllabus and study tracking
Chapters must persist permanently and never auto-delete
Study time tracked individually per chapter with timer functionality
Subject-Chapter hierarchy: Subjects contain multiple Chapters
Completed chapters remain visible but visually separated (e.g., faded or in a separate section)
Progress calculation: per-subject and overall completion percentages
Progress Report: aggregates subject and chapter statistics with visual bars
Study timer supports pause/resume in addition to start/stop functionality

existing_features.title: Implement user sign up functionality with email and password authentication
Implement user login functionality with email and password authentication
Create a dashboard page that displays the user's total syllabus progress as a percentage
Display total accumulated study time on the dashboard
Display counts of completed and pending chapters on the dashboard
Design a clean, modern dashboard interface with a cohesive visual theme using warm earth tones (terracotta, sage green, cream) with rounded components and subtle shadows for depth
Create a frontend page for the syllabus tracker that displays all user chapters in a list, showing chapter name, status badge, and individual study time for each chapter.
Add a form on the syllabus tracker page to manually create new chapters with a name input field and submit button.
Add status toggle functionality on each chapter item to switch between Pending and Completed states.
Create a study timer component with Start and Stop buttons that tracks elapsed time in real-time and displays it in minutes and seconds.
Add chapter selection to the study timer so users can associate timed study sessions with specific chapters, and save accumulated time to the selected chapter when timer stops.
Implement daily study goal setting functionality in the study timer, allowing users to set a target number of minutes for daily study.
Update the dashboard progress calculation to use actual chapter data: calculate percentage as (completed chapters / total chapters) × 100.
Update the dashboard chapter statistics to display actual counts from user's chapters: total chapters, completed count, and pending count.
Update the dashboard study time display to show the sum of all chapter study times from the user's actual chapter data.
Create a frontend page for managing subjects that displays all user subjects in a list with add subject form (subject name input and submit button)
Display a progress bar for each subject showing completion percentage calculated as (completed chapters / total chapters) × 100
Update the syllabus tracker page to organize chapters by subject, allowing users to select a subject first before viewing and adding chapters within that subject

Separate completed chapters from incomplete chapters in the chapter list, displaying completed chapters in a distinct 'Completed' section below incomplete chapters with tick mark and faded or highlighted styling
Update chapter status toggle to instantly reflect changes in the UI, moving chapters between incomplete and completed sections without page reload
Create a Progress Report component displaying: Total Subjects, Total Chapters, Completed Chapters, Remaining Chapters, Overall Completion Percentage, and a visual progress bar
Update the dashboard to display overall syllabus completion percentage calculated across all subjects using (total completed chapters / total chapters) × 100

Update the StudyTimer component to add pause and resume functionality: add a Pause button that appears when timer is running, and a Resume button that appears when timer is paused, preserving elapsed time across pause/resume cycles
Ensure the StudyTimer component tracks study time separately for each selected subject/chapter, maintaining accurate time accumulation per chapter when timer stops
Add a motivational quote display component to the dashboard that shows a different inspirational quote each day, placed prominently near the top of the dashboard page

new_feature_requests.summary: User authentication with email/password sign up and login
Separate private data storage per user
Dashboard showing syllabus progress percentage
Dashboard showing total study time
Dashboard showing completed and pending chapters count
Implement strict data isolation ensuring each user can only access their own private data
Syllabus tracker with manual chapter creation (name, status: Pending/Completed, permanent storage)
Study timer with start/stop, daily goal setting, and per-chapter time tracking

Create a backend data model for chapters with fields: chapter name (text), status (enum: Pending or Completed), and accumulated study time in minutes (nat). Chapters must be stored persistently and never auto-delete.
Implement backend methods to add new chapters with user access control, ensuring each user can only create and access their own chapters.
Implement backend methods to update chapter status between Pending and Completed, with user access control.
Implement backend methods to track and accumulate study time for individual chapters, storing time in minutes.
Add Subject entity: students can manually create subjects to organize chapters hierarchically
Chapters must belong to a Subject, allowing multiple chapters per subject
Display completed chapters separately (e.g., below or in a Completed section) with tick mark and faded/highlighted style
Calculate and display progress percentage per subject: (Completed Chapters / Total Chapters) × 100 with progress bar
Generate a simple Progress Report showing: Total Subjects, Total Chapters, Completed Chapters, Remaining Chapters, Overall Completion %, and visual progress bar
Update progress instantly when a chapter is checked or unchecked
Create a backend data model for subjects with fields: subject name (text) and creation timestamp, stored persistently per user with strict access control
Implement backend methods to create new subjects with user authentication, ensuring each user can only create and access their own subjects
Update the backend chapter data model to include a subjectId field, establishing a parent-child relationship where each chapter must belong to exactly one subject
Implement backend methods to query chapters by subjectId, returning all chapters belonging to a specific subject with authentication checks
Implement backend method to calculate per-subject progress as (completed chapters / total chapters) × 100, returning progress percentage for each subject
Add pause/resume functionality to the study timer (currently only has start/stop)
Display a daily motivational quote on the dashboard
